name: adsb_convergence
drl:
rule "converging"
when
    Pair(dist < 5000, closing == true, $b0_0 : key, $b0_1 : dist)
then
    insert(new Alert("converging", $b0_0, $b0_1));
end

rule "sustained"
when
    $p0 : Pair(dist < 5000, closing == true, $b0_2 : key)
    Pair(dist < 5000, closing == true, key == $b0_2, this after[1ms,10000ms] $p0, $b1_0 : key, $b1_1 : dist)
then
    insert(new Alert("sustained", $b1_0, $b1_1));
end
_finding: The MATCH-PLANE half of demo/adsb_convergence.py, byte-checked (D-249, the two-plane design): the derivation plane's exact outputs (haversine distances + closing flags from the polars stage, values verbatim from the demo run) asserted as Pair events; the certified subset does the rest with grammar it already had - two field constraints (dist < 5000, closing == true) plus this_after persistence. Fires converging at 4480m and 2998m, sustained on the second closing sub-5km pair. The match grammar gained ZERO productions for proximity alerting - that is the design's claim, and this cell is its receipt.
